channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Book Reports Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Privately Compared Trump to George Soros and Criticized Zelenskyy
item.description: A forthcoming book titled 'Regime Change,' written by New York Times reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, reportedly contains several revelations about Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's priva...

item.title: Opinion: Mark Kenny argues populist right-wing movements are showing signs of political weakness in Australia, the US and the UK
item.description: In this opinion piece, political analyst Mark Kenny argues that anti-establishment and populist movements across several Western democracies are beginning to show significant weaknesses. He contends t...

item.title: Philippines and United States Conclude Fourth Maritime Cooperative Activity in the West Philippine Sea for 2026
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and the United States military have successfully completed their fourth Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) in the West Philippine Sea this year, reinforcing ...

item.title: Nestory Irankunda and a Diverse New Generation of Socceroos Inspire National Pride Ahead of the World Cup
item.description: This episode of The Guardian's Full Story podcast focuses on Nestory Irankunda and the emerging generation of Socceroos players who have renewed optimism about Australia's prospects at the FIFA World ...

item.title: A Retrospective of Irving Penn’s Influential Vogue Cover Photography During the 1950s
item.description: This article highlights the remarkable Vogue magazine covers created by renowned photographer Irving Penn throughout the 1950s. Widely regarded as one of the most influential photographers of the twen...

item.title: Report on voting machines finds no evidence of fraud amid claims of delayed release by Trump administration
item.description: A former Republican congressman, Adam Kinzinger, has criticized the Trump administration over its handling of a government-commissioned report examining allegations of voting machine fraud. According ...

item.title: Lavrov Criticizes European Ukraine Policy and Calls for a New Eurasian Security Framework
item.description: The article reproduces a text attributed to Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov that was reportedly intended for publication in Politico Europe but was not ultimately published. In the article, Lav...

item.title: Critics dispute Trump's vandalism claim over Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation problems
item.description: A controversy emerged after President Donald Trump claimed that vandalism was responsible for problems affecting the recently renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C. Trump poste...

item.title: Congressman Criticizes DOJ Decision to End Environmental Investigation Into Senator Jim Justice's Coal Operations
item.description: Rep. Mike Levin (D-California) publicly criticized the Trump administration after reports emerged that the U.S. Department of Justice ended a federal criminal investigation into coal companies owned b...

item.title: Critics react to Trump’s proposal to rename ICE as “NICE”
item.description: Former President Donald Trump sparked online backlash after proposing a symbolic name change for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). In a Truth Social post, Trump suggested renaming the ag...

item.title: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif and CDF Asim Munir Travel to Switzerland for Iran-US Technical Talks
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Defence Forces Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir have left for Switzerland to join the technical-level talks between the United States and Iran in Bürgenstock. A...

item.title: Trump Suggests Possible U.S. Fees for Shipping Through the Strait of Hormuz if Ceasefire Agreement Fails
item.description: President Donald Trump proposed the possibility of imposing U.S.-controlled fees on ships traveling through the Strait of Hormuz if a recently established ceasefire arrangement in the region were to c...

item.title: Philippine Navy patrol vessel monitored by Chinese warships during West Philippine Sea mission near Bajo de Masinloc
item.description: A Philippine Navy patrol mission in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) faced close monitoring from Chinese naval forces near Bajo de Masinloc (Scarborough Shoal), highlighting ongoing maritime tensions in ...

item.title: Former Olympic Canoe Athlete Arrested After Incident at Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn, a three-time Olympic canoe slalom athlete, was arrested near the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., after allegedly touching a section of paint that had already begun p...

item.title: Legal Commentators Question Gorsuch's Historical Reasoning in Supreme Court Gun and Marijuana Case
item.description: The article discusses reactions from legal commentators Michael Popok and Lisa Graves to a unanimous U.S. Supreme Court decision in United States v. Hemani. The case involved a Texas gun owner who adm...

item.title: Anne Murray’s Stage Presence and Performance Style During the 1970s
item.description: The article looks back at Canadian singer Anne Murray’s live performances during the 1970s, a decade in which she became an international music star through hits such as “Snowbird,” “Danny’s Song,” an...

item.title: Environmental and Social Impacts of Data Centres on Local Communities
item.description: Data centres, which power internet services, cloud computing, artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency systems, are rapidly expanding across the world. While they are essential for the functioning o...

item.title: Japan Approves Significant Increase in Foreign Visa Fees Effective July 2026
item.description: The Japanese government has approved a substantial increase in visa application fees for foreign nationals, marking the first revision of these charges since 1978. The decision was formally adopted du...

item.title: Iran–US technical-level talks scheduled in Switzerland, FO confirms follow-up to Islamabad MoU
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan has announced that technical-level talks between the United States and Iran are scheduled to take place in Buergenstock, Switzerland, on Sunday. These discussions a...

item.title: Deepfake Doctors Exploit Healthcare Gaps and Public Distrust in Kenya
item.description: This opinion and analysis article examines the growing use of AI-generated deepfake doctors on social media platforms in Kenya and how they are being used to spread health misinformation and sell ques...
